description:
You are acting as an experienced PostgreSQL DBA who has just been given an empty, freshly-created Linux account (home directory /​home/​user).  
Your objective is to prepare the user’s shell environment for everyday query-tuning work and to keep a short, reproducible record of a tuning session.

Accomplish the following (5-10 shell commands will be enough):

1. Create a new file /home/user/.env_dba that permanently exports the four variables below (exact names, quotes and spacing matter):
   export PGDATABASE="salesdb"
   export PGUSER="analyst"
   export PGTZ="UTC"
   export PGOPTIONS="-c work_mem=64MB -c shared_buffers=256MB"

2. Ensure the *interactive* Bash shell loads those variables automatically.  
   • Append a clearly delimited snippet to the very end of /home/user/.bashrc that sources ~/.env_dba **only if the file exists**.  
   • Wrap the snippet between the two comment markers shown below.  The three lines (marker, if-block, marker) must appear verbatim so automated tests can find them:

   # >>> DBA ENV START >>>
   if [ -f ~/.env_dba ]; then
       . ~/.env_dba
   fi
   # <<< DBA ENV END <<<

3. Make a new directory called /home/user/db_tuning.

4. Inside that directory, create a session log named optimize_queries.log with the following exact layout (including blank lines, 10 ‘=’ symbols in each header bar, and no extra whitespace).  This log mimics the *before* and *after* EXPLAIN outputs and lists the steps taken in between.

========== BEFORE ==========
Seq Scan on sales (cost=0.00..431.00 rows=24000 width=12)

========== TUNING_STEPS ==========
1. Created index: CREATE INDEX idx_sales_date ON sales(order_date);
2. Adjusted work_mem to 64MB.
3. Enabled indexscan with: SET enable_seqscan = off;

========== AFTER ==========
Index Scan using idx_sales_date on sales (cost=0.43..123.00 rows=24000 width=12)

5. Verify your work by **printing (cat)** the following to the terminal in this order so the grader sees them:
   a. The contents of /home/user/.env_dba  
   b. The last 6 lines of /home/user/.bashrc (they should be exactly the snippet).  
   c. The full contents of /home/user/db_tuning/optimize_queries.log

No super-user privileges are necessary.  Ordinary default permissions (files 644, directory 755) are fine.  Remember: the formatting of the snippet and of optimize_queries.log must match the specification exactly so automated tests can compare them byte-for-byte.
truth:
Required end-state summary that the test harness will check:

1. File /home/user/.env_dba exists, mode 0644, with **exact** content:
export PGDATABASE="salesdb"
export PGUSER="analyst"
export PGTZ="UTC"
export PGOPTIONS="-c work_mem=64MB -c shared_buffers=256MB"
(final newline present)

2. The very last six lines of /home/user/.bashrc are exactly:
# >>> DBA ENV START >>>
if [ -f ~/.env_dba ]; then
    . ~/.env_dba
fi
# <<< DBA ENV END <<<
(final newline present)

3. Directory /home/user/db_tuning exists (mode 0755).

4. File /home/user/db_tuning/optimize_queries.log exists, mode 0644, with **exact** content including blank lines:

========== BEFORE ==========
Seq Scan on sales (cost=0.00..431.00 rows=24000 width=12)

========== TUNING_STEPS ==========
1. Created index: CREATE INDEX idx_sales_date ON sales(order_date);
2. Adjusted work_mem to 64MB.
3. Enabled indexscan with: SET enable_seqscan = off;

========== AFTER ==========
Index Scan using idx_sales_date on sales (cost=0.43..123.00 rows=24000 width=12)
(final newline present)

5. When the agent displays verification output, the order must be:
   • stdout dump of /home/user/.env_dba  
   • stdout dump of tail -n 6 /home/user/.bashrc  
   • stdout dump of /home/user/db_tuning/optimize_queries.log
